## How it Works

At [WolfSteel](https://wolfsteelbuildings.com/), we know starting a Texas Metal Building project can be overwhelming and extensive. Building, Concrete, Site Prep, Insulation, and so much more to look into can quickly turn into a headache. Our proven system guarantees you will have a smooth process from dirt to finish. We take on those headaches so you can enjoy your new Texas Metal Building.

That is our WolfSteel Difference.

- Design & Commit

- Permits & Planning

- Foundation Services

- Erection & Add Ons

![James Combs profile picture](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

[James Combs](https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/106068854262948963788/reviews)

01:28 11 Mar 25

I did a lot of research on steel building companies because my project was a little different than most; it was going to be built while I still lived in Hawaii and was not going to be around. I spoke to many companies who did not do concrete, or did not do doors, or did not do spray foam insulation, or couldn’t do my plumbing rough-in’s, and I needed an all-inclusive one stop shop to handle everything from start to finish. WolfSteel is able to do all this.

While speaking with building companies I also asked a lot of questions about structural integrity because I live in an area where we get strong straight line winds and many companies would answer with phases like “you can get away with \_\_\_\_” but when I spoke to WolfSteel, they put me at ease with phrases like “we require” or “our engineers require”. For my shop I did not want to “get away with”, I wanted it done right.

Other companies I spoke to also said they “bid out” or subcontract their installs (most likely to the lowest bidder), but WolfSteel has their own install crews and even their own tubular fab shop, so they have control over the quality of the builds and quality of their installs. Could I have saved some money going elsewhere? Probably, but I knew quality would have suffered and that was not an option for me. Why sacrifice quality to save a little money?

Overall the process was great, start to finish. Ernesto, my sales guy was extremely helpful and patient with all my changes along the way, and everyone I dealt with at WolfSteel was professional, patient, and very knowledgeable, always answering my numerous questions fully.

Wonderful experience and extremely happy with my 40x80 wide span tubular building.

The quote did not have the concrete at 3,500 sqft listed.

In the quote text, I was told that if I had any questions that we could have a call to go over the details, of which I agreed to; NEVER CALLED.

So then, I decided to call the next day after which time I drew a mockup of exactly what I wanted in hopes of having a more productive conversation. Conversation was anything but productive, just an intake person. So to recap everything in a timeline:

8/26 0830: Received initial text from Wolf

8/26 1330: Received pointless quote, nothing of what I asked for. Did not receive call as discussed via text.

8/27 1100: I call WolfSteel and provide them with a graphic of the project. of which I was told "We will get an updated quote to you either today or tomorrow".

8/28 1430: New person from Wolf reaches out asking about the doors I wanted. I said I wanted RESIDENTIAL DOORS.

8/29 1553: "Hi Casey. \*\*\*\*\* here with WolfSteel and my apologies for the late response. Just a quick update on my end, I'm still in the process of finalizing the quote for your project. I should have it ready and sent out by tomorrow."

8/30 ("Tomorrow"): Crickets

Labor Day weekend goes by..

9/3 0849: "Good morning Casey, it's \*\*\*\*\*\* here 🙂

Apologies for the late update. I just heard back from the team that the estimated cost for a 3500 concrete driveway is more or less $30k (may still change after a site visit). What do you think of this? Also, are you okay with increasing the leg height to 13' so we can fit the 2 roll-up doors on the front?"

After I literally said I do not want roll up doors (VIA TEXT), we are still talking about roll up doors. Also, the mockup that I sent over, calls for roughly 5,000 sqft. I had said 3,500 sqft. originally but concluded it would be closer to 5,000 after drafting a mockup and getting actual measurements.

So it was evident that no one looked at the mockup since they were still using the 3,500 number.

And what on earth does more or less $30k even mean? That could be $150k (more) or $15k (less). No sense of communication skills with this company whatsoever.

So in summary, my recommendation to this company is to take the time, effort, and money you put into your social media foot print and redirect it to proper training for your staff.

If this is how the beginning of the project was handled, I could only imagine the challenges we would have faced if we would have broken ground with this company.
